sections.paragraph:
γαστήρ, τρος, ἡ [cp. γέντα = ἔντερα ‘guts, intestines’; prim. ‘belly’, the inner region of the body containing, among other parts, the stomach and the womb]

—1. belly, in personifying imagery = glutton Tit 1:12.

—2. womb Lk 1:31; ἐν γαστρὶ ἔχειν be pregnant Mt 1:18; Mk 13:17; Lk 21:23; 1 Th 5:3; Rv 12:2.
